executive_summary: This report summarizes the technology performance metrics for Q2 2026 across the Infrastructure and Engineering division. Overall system reliability improved to 99.94% uptime, exceeding the 99.9% SLA target. API response times decreased by 18% following the cache layer rollout in March. Engineering velocity increased 12% quarter-over-quarter as measured by story points delivered. The division successfully completed 3 of 4 major milestones and remains on track for the H1 roadmap commitments.

sections.heading: Infrastructure Reliability
sections.body:
System uptime for Q2 2026 reached 99.94%, representing a 0.04 percentage point improvement over Q1. The primary driver was the completion of the automated failover project in late February, which eliminated the single point of failure in the primary database cluster.

Two incidents occurred during the quarter. The first, a 14-minute partial outage on March 8, was caused by a misconfigured load balancer rule deployed during a routine maintenance window. The second, a 6-minute degraded state on April 2, was traced to a network congestion event in the east region. Both incidents have documented post-mortems and corrective actions in progress.

subsections.heading: Incident Response Times
subsections.body: Mean time to detect (MTTD) improved from 8.2 minutes in Q1 to 4.7 minutes in Q2, a 43% reduction attributable to the enhanced alerting pipeline deployed in January. Mean time to resolve (MTTR) improved from 34 minutes to 21 minutes over the same period.

sections.heading: API Performance
sections.body:
Following the cache layer rollout on March 15, p50 API latency decreased from 42ms to 34ms (19% improvement). p99 latency decreased from 310ms to 248ms (20% improvement). These improvements are consistent across all three primary API clusters.

The read-through cache achieved a 73% hit rate in its first month of operation, exceeding the 65% target established at project kickoff. Cache invalidation events averaged 1,240 per hour with no observed stale-read incidents.

sections.heading: Engineering Velocity
sections.body:
The engineering organization delivered 1,847 story points in Q2, up from 1,648 in Q1 — a 12.1% increase. The improvement reflects the onboarding of three senior engineers in February and the completion of a 6-week toolchain modernization effort that reduced CI build times by 38%.

Deployment frequency increased from 4.2 per day to 6.8 per day. Change failure rate held steady at 1.4%, within the elite performance threshold defined by DORA metrics.

subsections.heading: Milestone Completion
subsections.body: Three of four H1 milestones were completed on schedule. The fourth milestone — federated identity rollout — is tracking 2 weeks behind plan due to an upstream dependency on a vendor API update. Revised completion target is May 14.

sections.heading: Cost & Resource Utilization
sections.body:
Cloud infrastructure spend for Q2 totaled $487,200, 3.2% under the $503,000 budget. Savings were driven by reserved instance purchases in January and rightsizing of 14 idle compute clusters identified during the March capacity review.

Storage costs increased 8% quarter-over-quarter, in line with projected data growth. No corrective action is required; the storage roadmap accounts for this growth through Q4.

sections.heading: Q3 Priorities
sections.body:
Key priorities for Q3 include: completing the federated identity rollout, beginning the database migration to the new managed service, expanding observability coverage to the three remaining legacy services, and conducting the annual disaster recovery exercise.

Capacity planning for the anticipated Q4 traffic increase is scheduled to begin in July. The infrastructure team will present a scaling proposal to leadership by July 31.
